de.uka.ilkd.key.symbolic_execution

Class ExecutionNodeReader.AbstractKeYlessExecutionBlockStartNode<S extends SourceElement>

  • java.lang.Object
    • de.uka.ilkd.key.symbolic_execution.ExecutionNodeReader.AbstractKeYlessExecutionElement
      • de.uka.ilkd.key.symbolic_execution.ExecutionNodeReader.AbstractKeYlessExecutionNode<S>
        • de.uka.ilkd.key.symbolic_execution.ExecutionNodeReader.AbstractKeYlessExecutionBlockStartNode<S>
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    IExecutionBlockStartNode<S>, IExecutionElement, IExecutionNode<S>
    Direct Known Subclasses:
    ExecutionNodeReader.KeYlessBranchStatement, ExecutionNodeReader.KeYlessLoopCondition, ExecutionNodeReader.KeYlessLoopStatement
    Enclosing class:
    ExecutionNodeReader

    public abstract static class ExecutionNodeReader.AbstractKeYlessExecutionBlockStartNode<S extends SourceElement>
extends ExecutionNodeReader.AbstractKeYlessExecutionNode<S>
implements IExecutionBlockStartNode<S>
    An abstract implementation of IExecutionBlockStartNode which is independent from KeY and provides such only children and default attributes.

    • Field Summary

      • Fields inherited from interface de.uka.ilkd.key.symbolic_execution.model.IExecutionNode

        INTERNAL_NODE_NAME_END, INTERNAL_NODE_NAME_START

    • Constructor Detail

      • AbstractKeYlessExecutionBlockStartNode

        public AbstractKeYlessExecutionBlockStartNode(IExecutionNode<?> parent,
                                              java.lang.String name,
                                              java.lang.String formatedPathCondition,
                                              boolean pathConditionChanged,
                                              boolean blockOpened)
        Constructor.
        Parameters:
        parent - The parent IExecutionNode.
        name - The name of this node.
        formatedPathCondition - The formated path condition.
        pathConditionChanged - Is the path condition changed compared to parent?
        blockOpened - false block is definitively not opened, true block is or might be opened.

      • isBlockOpened

        Checks if a block might be opened.
        Specified by:

        Returns:
